headings.text: Everything your team needs to ship words at the speed of code.
Built for the chaos of real product work.

paragraphs.text: Luminos plugs into the tools you already use and turns the messy reality of product work — half-finished Notion docs, cryptic Jira tickets, scattered Slack threads — into polished, customer-ready writing in seconds.
Point Luminos at an epic or a sprint label and it stitches every ticket, commit, and PR into a coherent release note draft.
No more copy-pasting summaries — the customer-facing prose writes itself, with proper grouping by feature, fix, and improvement.
Dial the same draft from terse engineering changelog to upbeat marketing email with three sliders: formality, hype, and technical depth.
Save tone presets per audience — your dev community gets the receipts, your enterprise buyers get the polish.
Approved draft? Push it directly to Confluence, Notion, your help center, or a scheduled customer email — formatting and links intact.
Version history syncs back, so future releases reuse the same structure and your docs stay in lockstep with shipping reality.
Luminos doesn't ask your team to change how they write Jira tickets or structure Notion docs. It reads the mess as-is, understands the context, and produces the artifact you actually need to ship.
Generate a 200-word changelog, a customer email, a tweet, and an internal status update — all from the same source — without leaving your sprint board.
Join 1,400 product teams already using Luminos to turn shipped code into customer-ready words — in seconds, not Fridays.
The AI writing copilot for product teams. Ship words as fast as you ship code.

Generate a 200-word changelog, a customer email, a tweet, and an internal status update — all from the same source — without leaving your sprint board.
luminos · v2.14 draft
## Highlights
• Faster CSV exports (3.2× on large datasets)
• New SSO via Okta and JumpCloud
• Dark mode for shared dashboards
## Fixes
• Resolved timezone drift on weekly reports
• Stabilized webhook retries on 5xx errors
— drafted from 14 Jira tickets · 2.3s
